def: s.f. = meṅḍ, or meṅṛ, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. = meṅḍuk, q.v.
= H مينڐکوي मेंडुकवि meṅḍukawi s.m. dialec. forms = meṅḍuk, q.v.
= H مينڐکا मेंडुका meṅḍukā s.m. dialec. forms = meṅḍuk, q.v.

ety: meṅg˚ = mīṅg or miṅg, prob. akin to S. मृज्, and मज्जा;+nī = lī = Prk. अडिआ or अलिआ = S. अ+ल+इका
def: s.f. The orbi. cular dung of goats, sheep, deer, camels, rats, &c.
s.m. Rain; a shower
subs: v.n. Rain to come, or to threaten
or meṅh paṛnā, v.n. Rain to fall; to rain

def: s.f. = مينہدي meṅhdī, q.v.

s.f. The Henna plant, or Broad Egyptian Privet, Lawsonia alba, or L. inermis, or L. spinosa (cultivated throughout India for its leaves, and as a hedge-plant for gardens; the powdered leaves, beaten up with catechu and made into paste are much used by women to dye their hands and feet a reddish-orange; and, by men, to dye their beards; and occasionally to stain the tails and manes of horses); — the marriage-feast on the occasion of the bride's hands and feet being stained with henna (ḥinā); — an ark, or tabernacle, carried in solemn procession by Muḥammadans on the eve of the anniversary of the death of a person who died just as he was about to marry
subs: (-meṅ), To apply henna (to, — as a cure for a blister, &c.)
or meṇhdī lagānā (-meṅ), To stain (the hands and feet, &c.) with henna; to apply henna (to).

def: s.f. corr. of ميخ meḵẖ, q.v.
v.n. To bleat (as a goat or sheep; — cf. S. meka, 'a goat').

ety: by redupl. of S. मे onomat.; and i.q. me-me
def: s.f. Bleating, bleat (of a goat or sheep)
s.f. A limit, limitary dike or bank; boundary-mound (of a field); a bank, an embankment; a dam, dike, mole, mound; a landmark; border, edge; hedge, fence; — a high wave, billow, surge (cf. meṅḍhā)
subs: s.f. A fixing or settling of boundaries; a record of boundaries

def: s.m. (dialec.) = meṅḍā, q.v.
s.f. The brim, or parapet, of a well.
s.m. (f. -ī), A ram (i.q. meṅḍhā, q.v.).
s.f. A limitary dike, &c. (i.q. meṅṛ, q.v.).

ety: me˚ = Prk. मइ, for S. मम, gen. of base अस्मद्;+rā = karā (gen. aff.), q.v.
def: adj. (gen. sing. of maiṅ; fem. merī), My, mine.

(n. of instr. fr. عرج 'to ascend'), s.f. & m. A ladder; a series of steps or stairs; anything by which one ascends; — the ladder upon which the souls ascend when they are taken from their bodies; the ladder upon which Moḥammad is related to have ascended from Jerusalem to Heaven; — (hence) ascent, ascension.
s.m. (f. -ī), A ram (i.q. meṅdhā).
s.m. = meṛhā, q.v.
s.f. The milky plant Odina pennata, or Asclepias geminata (the fruit of which is curved like a ram's horn).
adj. = merā, q.v.

ety: it is said to form the central point of Jambudvīp, all the planets revolving round it; and is compared to the cup or seed-vessel of a lotus, the leaves of which are formed by the different dvīps; its
def: s.f. Name of a daughter of Meru (wife of Nābhi and mother of Rishabha, who was an incarnation of Vishṇu).
s.m. Fragrant resin; incense.
s.m. (dialec.) = mis or mish, q.v.; — 2˚ s.f. corr. of mez, q.v.
s.m. A ram; sheep, &c. (see mesh).
s.m. = meshā, q.v.

ety: P. also mīsh; Pehl. mesh; Zend maesha; S. मेषः
def: s.m. A ram; sheep; — the sign Aries; a kind of drug
subs: (˚sha+an˚), s.m. (lit. 'having ram's testicles'), An epithet of Indra
mesh-pālak, or mesh-pālī, s.m. A shepherd
adj. & s.m. Sheep-eyed; sheepish; — one having eyes like a sheep's; (met.) a blockhead

def: s.m. Skin of a sheep or goat; kid (leather).
s.m. See s.v. mesh.
s.f. A ewe.

ety: S. मेष+इका; P. also meshī or mīshī
def: s.f. Chamois-leather; — uncoloured leather of sheep-skin or goat-skin; — parchment, vellum.
s.f. = meshī, q.v.
s.m. A mate; a maty boy, a scullion.

ety: S. मृत्तिका+कः, see miṭṭī, or S. मार्त्तिकः
def: s.m. (dialec.) An earthen water-jar, water-pot, pitcher (i.q. mīt, mītā).
v.n., and past conj. part. of meṭnā, q.v. (as a verb. noun it occurs chiefly at the end of compounds; — cf. the Maraṭhī meṭ).

ety: S. मृत्तिका+कः, see miṭṭī, or S. मार्त्तिकः
def: s.m. (dialec.) An earthen water-jar, water-pot, pitcher (i.q. mīt, mītā).
s.m. A ram (i.q. meṛhā, the com. form).
s.m. corr. of meṭā, q.v.
s.f. The plant fenugreek, Trigonellum fœnum-graecum (Pers. syn. shambalīt, or shimlīt)
s.f. = methī, q.v.

ety: meṭ, q.v.+ī or iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) A small earthen water-pot or jar; a small pitcher.

ety: meṭ, q.v.+ī or iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) A small earthen water-pot or jar; a small pitcher.

ety: meṭ˚ = Prk. मिट्ट(इ), or मिट्टे(इ), fr. मिट्ट, for मिट्ठ = S. मृष्ट, p.p.p. of rt. मृज्
def: v.t. To rub out, wipe out, blot out, efface, erase; to annihilate; to do away with, to annul, cancel; to atone for, to expiate; — to thwart, &c. (see the neut. v. miṭnā).
s.f. The myrobalan tree (syn. āmalakī); — the tree Spondias mangifera.
s.m. Name of a tribe inhabiting the mountainous province of Mewāt (it is much addicted to robbing).
s.m. corr. of ميوه mewa, q.v.
s.m. Fruit
subs: vulg. mewe-dār, adj. Fruit-bearing, fruitful; laden with fruit

def: (mewa+A. pl. suff. āt, with euphon. change of silent h of ميوه into j), s.m. pl. (of mewa), Fruits.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the province of Mewāt; — the robber-tribe of Mewāt (i.q. mew); a man of that tribe; — a native, or an inhabitant, of Mewāt.
s.m. = mew or meʼo, q.v.

ety: n. of instr. fr. عيار ěʻyār, q.v.
def: s.m. A standard of measure or weight; the mark or standard of money, &c.; — an assay-balance; — a touchstone.

ety: Pehl. mīj; Zend myaz; prob. akin to S. मह 'feast; sacrifice'; — cf. mez-bān and měhmān
def: s.f. A table
subs: or mez-mān, s.m. Landlord; master of the house, or of a feast; entertainer, host
To lay the table, or the table-cloth

def: s.m. See s.v. mez.
s.m. (dialec.) A buffalo (i.q. bhaiṅs, bhaiṅsā, q.v.).
pron. (dialec.) = merā, 'my, mine,' q.v.
s.m. = marhaṭā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = مہنا měhnā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) A variety of the meṅhdī, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = meṅhdī, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) A peacock (i.q. mor, q.v.).
s.f. (dialec.) = مہري mǒhrī, and موري morī, qq.v.

r: mīʻād, mīʻād baṛhānā, kī, mīʻād bolnā, mīʻād pūrī honā, mīʻād tamām honā, mīʻād-ě-g̠air-munqaẓiya, mīʻād kāṭnā, mīʻād-ě-muqarrara
def: (n. of place and time fr. وعد 'to promise'), s.f. & m. The time, or place, of a promise; fixed time, term, period (in Interest, or money-lending, &c.); usance; term of imprisonment
subs: (-kī), To extend or prolong a term
(-kī), To specify the term (of); to pronounce sentence of imprisonment for a term
or mīʻād tamām honā (-kī), A term or period to be complete, or to expire
(-kī), To pass the term or period (of); to undergo imprisonment for the full term

def: adj. For a time or term; periodical; limited as to time or place (as a lease, &c.), terminable; — s.m. & f. A term-prisoner
subs: s.m. A farm, or lease, for a specified term; a terminable lease

def: s.f. Death, decease; extinction.
v.t. (dialec.) = mićaulnā, q.v.
v.t. = mićrānā, q.v.

ety: mićaul(nā)+ī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य + ता + इका
def: s.f. Shutting or covering (the eyes)
subs: s.f. Shutting or covering the eyes; blindfolding; — the game of blind-man's-buff.

r: mićaulṇā, mić˚, mić-nā, aul˚, āwal, āw, al, āl
ety: mić˚, as in mić-nā, q.v.+aul˚ = āwal = āw+al ( = āl
def: v.t. To shut or cover (the eyes)
subs: (or āṅkheṅ) mićaulnā (-kī), To cover the eyes (of); to blindfold.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = mićar-mićar, q.v.
adj. &c. (old H.) = mithyā, q.v.

ety: mīćh˚, mīć˚ = Prk. मिच्छ(इ), or मेच्छ(इ) = S. मेक्ष्य(ति), future (used in sense of present) of rt. मिष्
def: v.t. To shut or close (the eyes); to wink (cf. mićnā).
v.t. To rinse.

ety: mīćh˚, mīć˚ = Prk. मिच्छ(इ), or मेच्छ(इ) = S. मेक्ष्य(ति), future (used in sense of present) of rt. मिष्
def: v.t. To shut or close (the eyes); to wink (cf. mićnā).
v.n. To be shut or closed (the eye); to shut, close; — v.t. = mīćnā, q.v.

ety: mićrā˚ = mićār˚ = S. मृक्ष+कार (or मृश + कार), cf. mić-kārnā, or ˚rā = ˚ār = āl, is the H. caus. augment
def: v.t. To play with food (by turning it over), to eat without appetite.
s.m. = midḥat, q.v.

ety: for A. مدحة, inf. n. of مدح
def: s.f. = madḥ, q.v.

ety: n. of instr. fr. فتح 'to open'
def: s.f. An instrument with which a lock is opened; a key.

ety: n. of instr. fr. غفر 'to cover,' &c.
def: s.m. Mail, or network of steel, worn under the helmet, or cap, or hat; — a helmet.
s.m. The magnet, loadstone (also written mag̠nat̤īs, or mag̠nīt̤īs, and mig̠nāt̤īs).

(n. of inst. fr. غال (for غول) 'to destroy'), s.m. lit. 'An instrument with which a thing is destroyed'; a knife, or a piece of iron, inserted in a whip or stick (which is as a sheath to it); — a long slender sword with a flat back; — a long spear-head with the end bent back; — a proper name (of a man).

ety: Zend maś, or maz; S. महा
def: adj. Great; chief, principal.

ety: fr. مہد 'to spread equally; to make (a bed
def: s.m. A bed; sofa; chair; throne; any place on which one sits or reclines.
s.m. See محافه muḥāfa.

ety: instrum. n. fr. حكّ 'to scratch, to rub,' &c.
def: s.f. The stone upon which pieces of money, &c. are rubbed to try their quality; touchstone; test.
s.f. pl. (of محنت měḥnat), Labours; troubles, afflictions, &c.

ety: mihā˚ = S. मेघ;; Prk. मेह+ā = āw = Prk. āwa, or āwe = S. आपि (caus. aug.); or = S. मिह्+ā, &c.
def: v.n. To become damp or humid.

ety: mih, q.v.+īn = Zend yaṅh = S. इयस् or यस्, nom. इयान् or यान्
def: adj. Fine, subtle; thin (not coarse); — greater, greatest; elder-born
subs: (or mahīn) āwāz, s.f. A fine or shrill sound; a high note; the treble; — a soprano voice; — a thin, or feeble, voice
s.m. A tailor; one who performs any fine needle-work

def: s.m. The sun; the moon; a cloud; wind, air (cf. next).

ety: Pārsī mihir; old P. mithra; Pehl. mitn; Zend mithra; S. मित्र
def: s.f. Love, affection, friendship, kindness, favour; mercy, pity, sympathy, feeling; — prosperity
s.m. The sun; — name of a Persian month in which falls the autumnal equinox (the month of September)

adj. Loving, affectionate, friendly, kind, benevolent, beneficent, favouring, indulgent, gracious, propitious; compassionate, merciful; — s.m. A friend; — mihrbānā, O friend! Dear Sir! — mihr-bān honā (-par), To be kind, or gracious, or favourable (to); to favour (with, as a visit, &c.); to feel pity (for), to take compassion (on).
s.f. (colloq.), corr. of next, q.v.

ety: mihrbān, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Friendliness, kindness, goodness, favour
subs: To show kindness (to), to treat kindly
(-par), To show friendship or kindness (to), to be kind or gracious (to), to confer favour (on), to favour, to befriend
It is your kindness or favour; it is very good or kind of you; thank you.

def: s.f. Affection, friendship, kindness (i.q. mihr; but only used as last member of compounds); — a kind of lute; — a psaltery, a sackbut.
s.f. See s.v. mihr.
s.m. corr. of مزاج mizāj, q.v.

ety: n. of place fr. جمر 'to give live coals to'
def: s.m. A vessel for fumigation; a censer; a chafing-dish; — incense; perfume.
v.t. = مينجنا mīṅjnā, q.v.
s.m. Lentil (i.q. masūr, q.v.); pease, pulse.
prop. n. The archangel Michael.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قطّ 'to cut'
def: s.m. The thing (generally a piece of bone or ivory) upon which the reed for writing is nibbed.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قدر 'to measure,' &c.
def: s.m. Measure; quantity, quantum; magnitude, size, bulk, dimension; proportion, extent; space; amount, sum, number attained; — rate; settled allowance; — term (of life)

ety: n. of instr. fr. کحل 'to anoint the eyes with kǒhl or "antimony"'
def: s.m. The instrument with which collyrium is applied to the eyes.
v.t. To beat, subdue, overcome.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قنع 'to turn downwards,' &c.
def: s.m. A veil of muslin or fine linen (esp. a bride's veil) worn over the head and reaching almost to the ground.

ety: for A. مقنعة miqnaʻat, fem. of miqnaʻ
def: s.m. A veil of muslin or fine linen (esp. a bride's veil) worn over the head and reaching almost to the ground.

ety: for A. مکنة, v.n. fr. مکن 'to occupy rank or station,' &c.
def: s.f. Authority, power, ability, might, strength, force.
s.m. The magnet, loadstone (also written mig̠nāt̤īs, q.v.; — syn. ćumbak).
wety: From Arabic مَغْنَاطِيس (maḡnāṭīs), ultimately of Ancient Greek origin. Akin to Pashto مقناطيس.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قرض 'to cut'
def: s.f. 'An instrument for shearing, or clipping, or cutting'; a pair of scissors; shears.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قطع 'to cut'
def: s.m. An instrument for cutting; shears (for cutting cloth, or silver, &c.).

ety: n. of instr. fr. قاس (for قيس
def: s.m. A measuring instrument, a measure; — a probe with which the depth of a wound is measured; — style (of a dial), gnomon; hand (of a watch); — the nilometer (of Egypt)
s.f. Probe (of a surgeon); a needle, bodkin, skewer, or wire (for anointing the eyes with collyrium; or, when heated, for blinding a person, by drawing it over the eyeballs); — barrel (of a gun).

ety: past. conj. part. of milnā, q.v.
def: part. = milkar or milke, q.v.

ety: A. also mīl, prob. fr. Lat. millia
def: s.m. A mile.

ety: perf. part. of milnā, q.v.; — and = Prk. मिलिअओ; S. मिलित + कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Mixed; united, &c.; met with, found, obtained, &c. (see milnā)
subs: or milā-jhulā, adj. (f. -ī), United; in harmony, harmonious; sociable; friendly
adv. In union, united; in harmony, harmoniously, on good terms, amicably; sociably, &c.
s.m. pl. (Physics) A system of forces.

def: part. = mil-ke or mil-karke (see milnā).
s.f. pl. of millat, q.v.

ety: S. मिलनं; — or i.q. milnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Coming into contact or union (with); contact; coalescence; union; mixing (with); agreement, harmony; meeting; encountering, &c. (see milnā).
s.m. The act of closing the eyes; winking, blinking, twinkling.

s.m. The bringing together or into contact; causing to mingle; forming a mixture; bringing about harmony, or reconciliation (between), adjustment (of differences, &c.), reconcilement, union; unison; producing harmony (in music); — comparing, comparison (of); — society, company; — host, army
subs: s.m. Specific gravity; — milān milānā (-kā), To adjust the accounts (of land under cultivation, &c.)

def: v.t. To cause to unite or join, &c.; to unite, join, connect, attach; to close; to cement; — to cause to meet or come together; to bring together; to introduce (one person to another); to act as a pander or a pimp between; — to put (into, or amongst); to put (together, or with); to mix, mingle, blend, combine; to cause to unite, or agree, or correspond, or harmonize; to assimilate; to reconcile; to adjust; to apply; to fit; to accord; to compare (with, -se)
subs: v.t. (intens.) To make friends of, to reconcile; to conciliate, to pacify; to persuade, to gain or win over, to make partizans of.

def: s.f. Mixing, mingling; adjustment; comparison, &c. (see milāʼo).
adj. Civil, affable, kind, &c. (i.q. milansār, q.v.); — faithful; worthy; — s.m. One who is sociable, or affable, &c.; — one who fraternizes (with others); an associate; a coadjutor.
adj. Civil, affable, kind, &c. (i.q. milansār, q.v.); — faithful; worthy; — s.m. One who is sociable, or affable, &c.; — one who fraternizes (with others); an associate; a coadjutor.
adj. Civil, affable, kind, &c. (i.q. milansār, q.v.); — faithful; worthy; — s.m. One who is sociable, or affable, &c.; — one who fraternizes (with others); an associate; a coadjutor.
adj. Sociable, friendly, familiar, intimate; civil, affable; convivial.

ety: milansār, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Sociableness, friendliness, affability, civility; geniality; concord, agreement, harmony.

s.m. = H ملاوا मिलावा milāwā [milā(nā) = āwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं], s.m. = H ملاوٿ मिलावट milāwaṭ [milā(nā)+āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं], s.f. Mixing, mingling, mixture; admixture; amalgamation; coalition; composition; annexation; adulteration; alloy; — union, concord, unison, accord; comparison; assimilation; reconciliation; adjustment; arbitration
subs: (-meṅ), To mix, mingle, intermingle; to alloy, to adulterate; to effect union or reconciliation (between), &c.

def: s.f. = milānī, and milāʼo, qq.v.

ety: prob. i.q. milāw or milāʼo, q.v.
def: s.m. (in Hindī) and f. (in Urdū), Mixing, mingling (with); meeting, interview, visit; social converse, society; — junction; coalescence; combination; union, agreement, harmony, concord, mutual good understanding, adjustment of differences; reconciliation; pacification, peace; — agreeing, suiting, consisting well together
subs: (-se), to agree or harmonize (with); to live in harmony or concord (with)
(-se), To harmonize; to make friends (with), to be reconciled (to)
(-meṅ), To bring about agreement or harmony (between); to effect a reconciliation (between), to reconcile, to make peace (between).

ety: milāp, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Sociable, friendly, intimate, affable, civil; convivial; — associate, companion; a social person, &c.

ety: mil(nā) + at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति (āpi, caus. augment+suff. ti)
def: s.f. Society, company; friendship

r: milaunī, milonī, milāwnī, milāʼonī, milāʼonā, milānā
ety: for milāwnī or milāʼonī, fem. of milāʼonā = milānā, q.v.
def: s.f. Water or other liquor added to make a mixture; — the mixture so made; — mixture, admixture; adulteration, alloy.

s.m. = H ملاو मिलाव milāʼo [milā(nā) + āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं], s.m. = H ملاوٿ मिलावट milāwaṭ [milā(nā)+āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं], s.f. Mixing, mingling, mixture; admixture; amalgamation; coalition; composition; annexation; adulteration; alloy; — union, concord, unison, accord; comparison; assimilation; reconciliation; adjustment; arbitration
subs: (-meṅ), To mix, mingle, intermingle; to alloy, to adulterate; to effect union or reconciliation (between), &c.

def: s.f. = H ملاو मिलाव milāʼo [milā(nā) + āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं], s.m. = H ملاوا मिलावा milāwā [milā(nā) = āwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं], s.m. Mixing, mingling, mixture; admixture; amalgamation; coalition; composition; annexation; adulteration; alloy; — union, concord, unison, accord; comparison; assimilation; reconciliation; adjustment; arbitration
subs: (-meṅ), To mix, mingle, intermingle; to alloy, to adulterate; to effect union or reconciliation (between), &c.

def: v.t. = milānā, q.v.
v.t. = milānā, q.v.
(v.n. fr. ملح 'to salt,' &c.), s.f.(?) Salt; — savouriness, palatableness; agreeableness; beauty, elegance.
part. (f. -ā), United, connected; mixed, combined; — found, obtained, met with, encountered.

s.f. Possession, dominion; property, right; landed property, estate (pl. amlāk); rent-free land; lands given to certain classes (as, 1˚ the learned and their pupils; 2˚ those who have abandoned the world; 3˚ the helpless and destitute; 4˚ decayed gentry; similar grants in money were termed waz̤īfa).

ety: conj. part. of milnā; — mil˚ = Prk. मिलिअ = S. मिलित्वा+kar (conj. part. of karnā), q.v.
def: part. Having united, having combined, &c.; — adv. In a body, in combination, in unity; together; unitedly; narmonionsly, on good terms
subs: v.n. To go, or journey together; — to live in harmony
To dwell together in unity or harmony.

ety: for A. ملکة, fr. milk, q.v.
def: s.f. Possession, property.
part. (of milnā), and adv. = milkar, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Proprietary; relating to ownership in land; — a landed proprietor, landlord; a farmer.

ety: for A. ملکية, abst. s. fr. milkī
def: s.f. Proprietary right in land; right; — landed or real property; property; possession, use.
s.m. = millat, q.v.

ety: for A. ملة, v.n. fr. ملّ 'to turn; to convert,' &c.
def: s.f. Religion, faith, creed; — a nation, people

ety: Prk. मिलणअं; S. मिलनीयं, rt. मिल्
def: v.n. To be mixed, be mingled, be blended; be confounded; be amalgamated; be shuffled; to be joined, be united; to become connected (with, -se); to be met with, be found, be obtained, be attained, to come to hand (to a person, us-ko), come into possession; — to mix, mingle, blend; to join, unite, combine; to come together, to come into contact (with); to meet; to touch; to take into the arms, to embrace; to associate (with); to visit, to have an interview (with); to assemble; to coincide (with), to coalesce (with); to agree, accord; to be reconciled (to); to harmonize; to correspond; to suit, fit, tally; to occur
subs: v.n. To live together in harmony or unity
v.n. To mix together; to come together; to have an interview (with); to be met with, be found, be obtained; to be recovered; to hold together, &c. (see milnā, of which it is generally intensive)
v.n. To be regularly or constantly obtained or met with; — to unite and live with; to live in harmony (with)
or milnā-jhulnā, v.n. To meet cordially; to have unrestricted intercourse (with)
To get the intended presents ready
v.n. To be together; to act together; to join, combine, coalesce
To go to meet or receive; to go on a visit (to, -ko).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀺𑀮𑀤𑀺 (miladi), from Sanskrit मिलति (milati).

def: s.f. A meeting, an interview; a visit; reception; — a present made by a visitor, or by the relations to a bride.

ety: imperf. part. of milnā; and = Prk. मिलंतअं = S. मिलन्त+कं
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Mixing; agreeing, harmonizing, &c. (see milnā)
subs: or miltā-jhultā, adj. (f. -ī), Partially agreeing or harmonizing; resembling partially

def: v.t. To cause to be mixed, &c.; to cause to be joined or united; to cause to be reconciled, &c.; — also = milāṇā, q.v.
the letter م m (which, in reckoning, stands for 40).

r: mīm, m., ma, abjad, Mǒḥarram., ma-kār
ety: the twenty-eighth of the Persian alphabet, and twenty-fourth of the Arabic
def: s.m. The letter, or the sound ma.
s.f. Reflection, consideration, opinion; investigation of truth; — name of one of the systems of Hindū philosophy.
s.m. An investigator of truth; — a follower of the Mīmāṅsā philosophy (esp. of the Pūrva-mīmāṅsā system).
s.m. Member

ety: n. of instr. fr. نبر 'to elevate, raise up'
def: s.m. Pulpit; rostrum; high chair; reading-desk.
adj. Possessing, or beginning with, the letter mīm (as an inf. n.)
subs: s.m. An infinitive noun beginning with the letter mīm or m (as mabīt, q.v.).

ety: mīmiyā˚ = me-me, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwa or āwe = S. आपि caus. aug.
def: v.n. To bleat (as a lamb, or kid; — i.q. mimiyānā, q.v.).

ety: mimiy˚ = me-me (onomat.)+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To bleat (as a goat, or sheep).
prep. From; of; out of; on, upon (syn. az, and -se)
subs: adv. From the side (of), from; on the part (of); by
adv. Among, among all; in all; from among all or the whole, out of the whole (of); from among; out of; — upon the whole; — totally, universally
adv. As it is, or was; circumstantially; to the very letter
s.m. (lit. 'from that, therefrom') Subtraction; deduction; — part. adj. Subtracted; deducted
v.t. To subtract, or deduct (from, se), to remit
adj. Subtracted, deducted; — s.f. An abatement, a reduction; — a reduction in assessment; — remission of rent or revenue; — unassessed land.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian من (man), ultimately from Akkadian 𒈠𒈾 (manûm).

def: s.f. (in H., but m. in S.), A fish; — the sign Pisces; — name of the first of the ten incarnations or manifestations of Vishṇu (i.q. matsya, or matsyāvatār)
subs: s.m. 'Fish-bannered'; an epithet of Kām-dev, the god of love
(-meṅ), 'To fish out faults or defects,' to criticise; to be hypercritical.

def: s.m.(?) Name of a valley near Mecca where sacrifices are offered up.

ety: also mīnū; old Pers. mīnô; Pehl. minôi; Zend mainyu (fr. man); S. मन्यु; — cf. also Zend minu; and S. मणि
def: s.m. Heaven, paradise; the sky, the azure vault; — a blue colour; — a false gem of a blue colour; a glass globule or bead; — the blue-stone, blue vitriol; caustic; a stone resembling lapis lazuli (which is used to tinge silver); — aichymy; — enamel; — a goblet, glass; decanter
subs: s.m. A bāzār where miscellaneous goods are sold; a fancy fair

def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs (of Rājpūtanā) who are professedly thieves.
adj. Of enamel, enamelled; — s.f. Enamel.
s.f. pl. of minnat, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. mīn.

ety: fr. A. manār, by imāla; — mīnārā = P. mīnāra = A. manārat
def: s.m. A tower, turret, steeple, spire, minaret; a pillar, column, an obelisk; — a boundary-pillar; a mile-stone; — (met., in the language of women, and corr. to munārā) a burden (as an unmarried daughter).

ety: fr. A. manār, by imāla; — mīnārā = P. mīnāra = A. manārat
def: s.m. A tower, turret, steeple, spire, minaret; a pillar, column, an obelisk; — a boundary-pillar; a mile-stone; — (met., in the language of women, and corr. to munārā) a burden (as an unmarried daughter).
v.t. (dialec.) = mīṅjnā, q.v.

ety: n. of instr. fr. نفخ 'to blow, puff, pant,' &c.
def: s.m. A pair of bellows.
s.f. = mīṅgī, q.v. (and cf. next).
s.f. = mīṅgī, q.v. (and cf. miṅgnī).

ety: mīṅg˚ = meṅg˚, as in meṅgnī+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Marrow (i.q. majjā); kernel; pith; essence (syn. gūdā; med).
s.f. = meṅgnī, q.v.
s.m. 1˚ = meṅh, q.v.; — 2˚ = meh, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. min.
adj. &c. (fr. min-hā), See s.v. min.
(fr. نہج 'to point out, or make clear, a road or way,' &c.), s.m.(?) An open or broad road; a straight road; a highway.
adv. See s.v. min.

ety: mīṅj˚ = Prk. मिंज(इ) = S. मृञ्ज(ति), rt. मृज्
def: v.t. To rub with the hands; to crush, pulverize; to rub, scrub, scour, clean (i.q. māṅjnā, q.v.).
adv. See s.v. min.

ety: n. of instr. fr. نقب 'to dig (through a wall, &c.
def: s.m. The instrument with which a person is tapped for dropsy, a trocar.

ety: n. of instr. fr. نقر 'to strike; to peck,' &c.
def: s.f. Beak, bill (of a bird; — syn. ćoṅć).

ety: n. of instr. fr. نقش 'to carve, engrave; — to pull out (a sting, &c.
def: s.m. Pincers; tweezers.
s.m. = minqāsh, q.v.

ety: n. of inst. or place fr. نخر 'to snort,' &c.
def: s.f. A nostril.
(obl. dual of minḵẖar), s.f. dual, The nostrils.
adj. & adv. (dialec.) Speaking indistinctly; speaking in a low or under-tone; murmuring; — burning dimly or faintly, glimmering, flickering (a light); — faintly; dimly; slowly.
adj. & adv. (dialec.) Speaking indistinctly; speaking in a low or under-tone; murmuring; — burning dimly or faintly, glimmering, flickering (a light); — faintly; dimly; slowly.

ety: minminā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. aug.+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. (dialec.) To speak indistinctly, or in a low tone, or with a feeble voice; to murmur; — to burn dimly or feebly (a light), to glimmer, to flicker; — to eat slowly.
part. adj. (fem. minnā; rt. mid), Become fat; fat; — unctuous; — moist; — affectionately disposed (towards).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian من (man), ultimately from Akkadian 𒈠𒈾 (manûm).

ety: for A. منة, inf. n. of منّ 'to confer a favour (on
def: s.f. Kindness or service done (to); favour, obligation; — grace, courtesy; — entreaty, humble and earnest supplication; — grateful thanks, praise
subs: v.t. To put (one) under an obligation, to oblige
s.f. The being under an obligation
adj. & s.m. Sensible of obligations, grateful; — one who is sensible of obligations, &c.
(-se), To ask a favour (of), to make earnest supplication, to importune; — to confer a favour (on, -par), to oblige

def: s.m. A cat (prob. fr. the mewing, miyāʼoṅ).
v.t. = mīṅḍnā, q.v.

ety: n. of instr. fr. نشر 'to spread, to disperse, scatter,' &c.
def: s.m. A saw, hand-saw; — a wooden pronged instrument (for winnowing corn); — a fan; a ventilator.

ety: for A. منطقة mint̤aqat, n. of instr. fr. نطق
def: s.m. A girdle, zone, belt, sash (syn. kaṭi-bandh)

ety: i.q. bintī; S. विनति+का, rt. नम् with वि
def: s.f. Solicitation, entreaty
s.m. Heaven, paradise; — an emerald; — white or blue glass; — a glass gem, or bead, &c. (see mīnā).
s.m. Mode, manner, form, rule.
s.m. Chief, leader, master, head, dux; a title by which the Saiyids (or descendants of the family of Muḥammad) are called; — (in cards) the king
subs: or mīr-ātish, s.m. Chief of the fireworks; commandant of artillery, master of the ordnance
vulg. mīr-āḵẖur or āḵẖor, s.m. Master of the horse
s.m. An admiral; — an overseer of boats; — a collector of port-duty, a harbour master; — mīr-baḥrī, s.f. Admiralty; — port-dues
s.m. Marshal; — an officer who maintains order in a march or procession; master of the ceremonies
or mīr-daha, s.m. A commander, or superintendent, of ten; a decurion; — a tithing-man
s.m. Master of the hunt, chief huntsman; — grand falconer; — a bird-catcher; (met.) a pimp
or mīr-ʻadl, s.m. Chief justice; superintendent of the courts of justice (who revised the decisions of the Qāẓīs, &c., and passed sentence)
s.m. A stone, or weight, placed on the edge of a carpet or the like, to keep it down
or mīr-majlis, s.m. Master of the ceremonies; — president, or chairman
s.m. Headman of a quarter (of a town)
s.m. Chief of the kitchen; chief cook
or mīr-manzil, s.m. Overseer of the halting-places; — quartermaster-general
s.m. Chief secretary; — head (native) clerk (of an office).

def: s.m. corr. of مير mīr, q.v.
pron. adj. m. (f. -ī), contrac. of ميرا merā, q.v.

ety: mīr, q.v.+ān = Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: s.m. A portion paid to the head of a department out of the fees received by the inferior officers; — name of a saint and miracle-monger (whose memory is held in veneration by women, and the lower orders of certain classes of Muḥammadans and Hindūs)
subs: s.m. The saint Mīrān; — the month rabīʻu-s̤-s̤ānī (in the language of women)
prov. 'Mīrān's grave (is) as (or as large as) Mīrān'; expenditure as the income.

ety: n. of place fr. ورِث 'to receive by inheritance'
def: s.f. Inheritance, heritage, hereditary estate, patrimony; a bequest.

ety: fem. of mīrās̤ī, q.v.; — an = in = S. इनी
def: s.f. A singing-girl (of a caste who sing only before women).
adj. Inherited; hereditary; — s.m. A singer (of a caste which follows that profession from generation to generation).

ety: for A. مرْآة, orig. مرْأَية, fr. رأي 'to see'
def: s.f. A mirror; a speculum
subs: s.f. The mirror of the invisible world, or of futurity.

def: s.f. Pepper; black pepper; — a chilli, Capsicum frutescens
subs: 'To feel as if chillies were applied,' to smart, to tingle, to burn; — to take ill or amiss
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit मरीच (marīca), from मरिच (marica).

def: s.m. = mirć, q.v.
= H مرچاني मिर्चानी mirćānī [mirćā, q.v.+nī = S. इनी], s.f. dialec. forms, = mirć, and mirćā.
= H مرچائي मिर्चाई mirćāʼī, मरिचाई marićāʼī [˚ī = S. इका], s.f. dialec. forms, = mirć, and mirćā.
s.f. (dialec.) = mirć, q.v.
s.m. A caste of migratory shepherds in the south of India; — an individual of that caste.
s.m. One who plays on the mr̤idang, a drummer.
s.m. One who plays on the mr̤idang, a drummer.
s.m. An officer employed by government in villages; an overseer; an exciseman; — one who carries the chain in surveying.

ety: n. of instr. fr. رفق 'to do (a work, or thing
def: s.m. A thing by which one profits, or gains advantage or benefit; advantage, gain, profit; — an appurtenance; a convenience; — the elbow.
s.f. Epilepsy; falling-fits
subs: (-ko), To be subject to epilepsy, to have epileptic fits

ety: mirgī, q.v.+Prk. इओ (with h inserted) = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Afflicted with epilepsy, epileptic; — an epileptic subject.
adj. & s.m. = mirgīhā, q.v.
s.m. One who surpasses or outstrips another (in any business, or game, &c.); a winner (at play); — (among schoolboys) he that first comes to the master to say his lesson.
s.f. corr. of mirzaʼī = mirzāʼī, q.v.

ety: mir˚ = mīr = amīr, q.v.+o = P. rav or rau; or = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि, caus. augment
def: v.n. (dialec.) To proceed with state and grandeur; to make a tour; to perambulate, stroll, roam.
s.m. Winner (at a game), dux, head, &c.
subs: To remain (or to be) winner, or dux, &c.

def: s.m. The planet Mars (syn. jallād-ě-falak; maṅgal).
adj. &c., corr. of mithyā, q.v.
s.m. 'Son of a great lord'; a prince, &c. (i.q. mirzā, q.v.)
subs: adj. Of princely disposition; noble-minded; genteel.

r: mirzā, mīrza, mīr, amīr, zā, ẓāda
ety: for mīrza; mīr = amīr+zā = ẓāda, qq.v.
def: s.m. A Persian or Mogal title; a prince (when it is put after a proper name, it usually means 'prince'; as Ibrahīm mirzā, Prince Ibrahīm; but when it appears before, it means a gentleman only, as mirzā Ibrahīm, Mr. Ibrahīm)
subs: s.m. A reckless fellow; — three small delicate golden chains, worn as the ṭīkā, fixed to the hair by small hooks (the lower ends being sometimes set with precious stones)
adj. Of a princely nature or disposition; genteel.

ety: mirzā, q.v.+ī = Zend ī = S. इ
def: s.f. The behaviour or manners of a mirzā; gentility; princeliness; princedom; — arrogance; — a cotton or muslin jacket with long sleeves and open cuffs worn under the qabā; a quilted coat (pop. mirzaʼī).
s.f. Princeliness, nobility, gentility, &c. ( = mirzāʼī and mirzaʼī, qq.v.).
s.f. An under-jacket, &c. ( = mirzāʼī, q.v.).
s.m. Copper (syn. tāṅbā)
subs: s.m. A worker in copper, a coppersmith; a brasier.

def: s.m. Pretence, pretext, excuse; sham; stratagem; false appearance, assumed form; — postp. In the form or character of; as; as to (e.g. phūpī-mis lījiye, bhatīje-mis dījiye, 'take as the aunt, give as to the nephew')
subs: To make a pretext or excuse, to pretend, &c.

def: s.m. A promise, an agreement, a bargain, compact, covenant; a confederacy, an alliance, a league
subs: s.m. Agreements, articles, or conditions (of peace, &c.).

ety: v.n. fr. مثل 'to compare, liken,' &c.; see mis̤l
def: s.f. Likeness, similitude; simile; analogy; parable, metaphor; specimen, example, model; a case adduced as a precedent; — adj. Like, resembling
subs: To give an example or illustration
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مِثَال (misāl), borrowed from Arabic مِثَال (miṯāl).

def: adj. Like, resembling.
s.m. A soil consisting of a mixture of clay and sand.

ety: inf. n. of ماسس 'to be in contact with, to touch,' iii of مسّ 'to touch'
def: s.m. Touching; touch; mutual contact; pressing; lying with; coitus.
s.f. An unmarried girl or lady (of European parentage).

ety: n. of instr. fr. صبُح 'to be or become bright'
def: s.f. A light, lamp, lantern (cf. sirāj).

ety: n. of instr. fr. صدق 'to prove true or correct'
def: s.f. Whatever confirms or proves the truth (of a thing); a verifier.
s.m. See s.v. mis.

s.m. Rivalry, emulation; envy; — deception, deceit, fraud, trick, stratagem; sham, pretence; excuse, apology; — illusion, false appearance (i.q. mis, q.v.); — adv. Under the pretence or pretext (of, -ke); having assumed the form (of).

ety: for A. مشربة mishrabat, n. of instr. fr. شرب 'to drink'
def: s.m. A drinking-vessel (syn. piyāla).
part. adj. (f. -ā), Sprinkled, moistened; — sweet, sugary; pleasant, agreeable; nice, savoury, dainty, delicate; — s.m. A sweetmeat; — a dainty or savoury dish
subs: (˚ṭa+an˚), s.m. Sweetmeat; sweet or savoury food, a savoury dish; frumenty; a mixture of sugar and acids eaten with rice or bread; sauce, gravy, seasoning.

def: s.m. See s.v. mishṭ.
adj. f. Made of vetches (māsh).
adj. Made of copper; — s.f. = missī, q.v.
s.f. corr. of ميشی mīshī, q.v.
s.f. A sort of fennel, Anethum sowa, and A. panmori; — common anise, Pimpinella anisum; — spikenard, Nardostachys jatamansi (also written miśi; miśī; mishi).
s.f. = misi, or misī, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. ثقل 'to be heavy or weighty'
def: s.m. A weight of 1 3⁄7 drachms, or of 4 māshās and 3½ rattīs (q.v.s.v. tolā).
(n. of instr. fr. صقل 'to polish'), s.m.(?) A polishing-instrument (com. a bead-shaped stone, or a smooth shell).
s.m. = miṣqal, q.v.
s.m. = miṣqal, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Lowly, humble, submissive; meek, mild; — poor, needy, indigent, wretched, miserable; — a poor man or woman, a pauper, a miserable wretch.
s.f. Lowliness, humility, submissiveness; meekness, mildness; — poverty, indigence, misery (syn. maskanat).
adj. Like, resembling; alike, similar, analogous (to); — s.f. Likeness, similitude; — a bundle of papers on one case, a file
subs: s.f. A file of miscellaneous proceedings
(-kī), To file the proceedings (of a case), to prepare or arrange the file (of a case)
or mis̤l bannā (-kī), The file of proceedings (of a case) to be prepared or arranged
s.f. The file or record of a case
or shāmil-ě-mis̤l karnā, To file with the record (of the case), to place on the record.

def: adj. Like, similar; — s.m. One whose name is on the file, an old offender; — s.f.(?) An article which being lent or sold is to be replaced by another exactly similar.
s.m. A nail, pin, peg, stake (of iron); — (in Pers. & Hind.) adj. Razed, demolished, destroyed
s.f. Razing; demolition, ruin, destruction.

ety: mis˚ = Prk. मिस(इ) = S. मृश(ति), rt. मृश्
def: v.n. To be pulverized, to be ground, crushed, or triturated, &c.; — to be rumpled, or crumpled (by rubbing).
v.t. To grind, pulverize; — to tweak, to twitch; — to rumple, crumple.
s.m. A city, a large town; — Egypt.
ex: مصر جانے کو جی مچلتا ہے
My heart yearns to go to Egypt
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مِصْر (misr), from Arabic مِصْر (miṣr).

def: s.m. corr. of مصر miṣr, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = مشر miśr, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Mixed, mingled, blended, joined, united; — compound; — a title of respect (used at the end or beginning of a proper name, especially that of a scholar); a respectable man, gentleman; Sir; a title of Maithila Brāhmans; a title of Brāhmaṇs who were brought to India from Śākadvīp by Krishṇa (to cure the leprosy of his son Śamba); a Hindū physician; — an elephant of the fourth of the four classes into which elephants are divided; — (fr. S. miśram) a mixing, mixture
s.m. corr. of مصراع miṣrāʻ, q.v.
s.m. Either half, or leaf, of a folding door; — (in poetry) a hemistich, a single line (of a poem).
s.m. Either half, or leaf, of a folding door; — (in poetry) a hemistich, a single line (of a poem).

ety: for A. مصرعة miṣraʻat, n. of un. fr. miṣraʻ
def: s.m. = miṣrāʻ, q.v.
s.m. The wife of a Miśr; a title of respect.
s.f. The wife of a Misr or Miśr, q.v.

adj. Mixing; adulterating; — mixed, mingled; compounded, compound; miscellaneous; adulterated; — (f. -ā) a mixer, a compounder; an adulterator (of articles of commerce); (S. mishrakam), salt produced from salt soil; — a particular kind of song or singing; — name of a tīrth (i.q. Naimish); — name of a grove or garden of paradise.
s.m. Mixing, mingling; uniting; (in Arith.) addition.
s.f. The wife of a Misr or Miśr, q.v.
s.m. The wife of a Miśr; a title of respect.
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to Egypt, Egyptian; — a native of Egypt, an Egyptian; — s.f. Egyptian sugar, sugar-candy; — a hunting-knife; a kind of sword
subs: A cup-shaped lump of sugar-candy; — (met.) a sweet melon
(-ko), To give (one) a taste of the miṣrī or 'sword,' to stab; — to betroth (by giving sugar-candy to eat).

def: adj. &c., corr. of مصري miṣrī, q.v.
s.f. = misrānī, q.v.
part. = مشرت miśrit, q.v.
part. (f. -ā), Mixed, mingled, blended, united, joined; added; — respected; respectable.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Mixed, mingled; — mixed grain, &c.
subs: or missā-kuskā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Mixed, mingled, blended; miscellaneous; — coarse; poor, refuse (food); — a mixed collection (of things), odds and ends; refuse.

ety: Prk. मिस्सिआ; S. मिश्रिता or मिश्र+इका, lit. 'a mixture, or compound'
def: s.f. A powder (composed of yellow myrobalan, gall-nut, iron-filings, vitriol, &c.) used for tinging the teeth of a black colour
subs: s.m. A box (of gold, silver, or other metal) for holding missī
To apply missī to the teeth and kājal or 'lamp-black' to the eyes, to beautify oneself
or missī-lagānā, or missī malnā (-meṅ), To mark lines with missī (on the teeth); to apply, or to rub, missi (to, or on, the teeth).

ety: prob. S. मृष्ट+कः, rt. मृज् or मृश्
def: s.m. (dialec.) A place made smooth and level (for treading out corn); — a desert without herbage.

ety: v.n. of instr. fr. سطر 'to rule' (a book
def: s.f. An instrument with which a book is ruled (made of a piece of pasteboard with strings strained and glued across it, which is laid under the paper; the latter being ruled by being slightly pressed over each string); — a ruled line, a line.

ety: prob. corr. fr. the English 'master,' pron. mistar
def: s.m. A head artificer, a master mason, or bricklayer, &c.

ety: n. of instr. fr. ساك (for سوك
def: s.f. A tooth-stick, tooth-brush, dentifrice (a piece of stick with which the teeth are rubbed and cleaned, the end being made like a brush by beating or chewing so as to separate the fibres; i.q. dāṅtaun, which, in India, is com a stick of the nīm tree).
s.m. (dialec.) contrac. of mīt, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

def: s.m. A friend; lover (i.q. mītā).
s.m. A pitcher; a cup, &c. (i.q. mītā, q.v.).
s.m. (f. -ī), Friend; lover, sweetheart; — a namesake.
s.m. A pitcher; — a porringer, a cup (dialec. forms are meṭ, meṭā).
part. Measured off, measured out; defined, limited, bounded; restricted; moderate; regular; scanty, frugal, sparing
subs: mitāśī (˚ta+aś˚), adj. & s.m. Eating little or sparingly, moderate in diet, abstemious; — an abstemious person; — (mitāśan) s.m. Moderation in eating, abstemiousness
(˚ta+ak˚), s.f. (lit. 'having measured syllables, metrical'), Name of various concise and celebrated commentaries of the Hindūs
adj. Of measured speech, speaking little; prudent or deliberate in the use of speech.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

ety: mīt, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Friendship, &c. ( = mitrāʼī and mitratāʼī, qq.v.).
v.t. (dialec.) = next, q.v.
s.f. A female friend, or companion, a sweetheart; — a female namesake.
